BlinkOps provides security operations automation, using artificial intelligence (AI) to improve security processes. Its main offerings include a security operations platform integrating AI-driven micro-agents and workflow automation for security incident response and management. It primarily serves sectors requiring security measures, including information technology (IT) security, cloud security, and governance and compliance. results.title: Top Tines Competitors and Alternatives for 2026 - Strike48
results.content:
Tines delivers genuine cross-functional workflow automation at enterprise scale. Deployments across Snowflake, Coinbase, GitLab, and Canva prove it works. The AI Interaction Layer launched in January 2026 added unified AI orchestration with MCP server support, human-in-the-loop controls, and audit logging for every AI action. Two-thirds of Tines customers now actively use AI features in the platform. [...] The data foundation column determines whether any AI or agentic capability downstream can be trusted. Every platform that inherits existing gaps automates the alerts it can see. Whether that covers the full environment is a question each team needs to answer before selecting a platform. If the answer is "we monitor roughly two-thirds of our log sources," then faster automation over two-thirds coverage still leaves one-third of the attack surface uninvestigated.

## How to Choose a Tines Alternative

Different problems point to different answers.

If the problem is workflow flexibility across teams (security, IT, HR, operations), Tines remains a strong answer. No platform in this list matches its cross-functional breadth. [...] Q: Is Tines a SOAR platform? A: Tines is often evaluated alongside SOAR platforms but positions itself as a broader workflow automation tool. It lacks some of the security-specific case management, pre-built security playbook libraries, and detection-layer integrations that purpose-built SOAR platforms provide natively. For teams that need deep SOC-specific agent behaviors or compliance evidence collection out of the box, dedicated security platforms cover more without requiring custom builds.

results.title: GitHub - msphn/Tine-2.0-Open-Source-Groupware-and-CRM: Tine 2.0 main repository · GitHub
results.content:
## Support

In general, users of the Community Edition are technically experienced and able to help themselves. The ability to read,
understand and change the software as well as to share the new version with others really is the heart of free software.

Moreover, in our Issues and Questions Section
you can get support from other community members. As a rule of thumb: You can expect more support from others the more
you contribute yourself. Don't expect others to contribute if you are not willing to contribute yourself. Remember: We
are a community of free software enthusiasts and innovators. If you are out for a good Samaritan you need to look
elsewhere. [...] There is no commercial support for the community edition at all. Please be fair and don't try to suck community
resources if you are not willing or able to contribute to the community. In this case the community edition is not
appropriate for you, please go with the business edition.

## Business Edition

For all those who are not willing or able to be part of the community as an active contributor, and for organisations
who prefer stability and support over innovation we created our Tine 2.0 Business Edition.

Once a year we take a subset of well proven and stable applications and features from our community edition and build
a new major release of our business edition. [...] | Name | | Name | Last commit message | Last commit date |

results.title: Tines | Intelligent workflow platform
results.content:
workflow.live
One day. Six sessions. Live conversations with leaders transforming their organizations with AI.

### What is an intelligent workflow?

### Unlocking AI’s full value:How IT orchestrates secure, scalable innovation

## Unmatched time to value

Teams love Tines for our speed, power, and ease of use.

An opportunity to bring automation into areas of the organization that just don’t have developers.

No matter how many tools you change, you can keep Tines in place.

My favorite thing about Tines is how easy and user-friendly it is. For me, it feels like a game. Whenever I build in Tines, I feel like I'm solving a fun puzzle.

750 daysof work time saved [...] ### The intelligent workflow difference

Too often, valuable work is constrained by outdated systems, fragmented tools, and inaccessible data. Tines creates the foundation to deploy intelligent workflows – from fully agentic to human-driven – that drive business impact.

#### Intelligent workflows

The secure, reliable integration layer that connects teams, tools, and data.

#### Vendor-agnostic

Secure integration across your stack including LLMs, MCPs, and internal tools.

#### For the whole team

Intuitive, flexible ways for the whole team to build and collaborate across workflows.

#### Born in security

Governance, guardrails, and monitoring across every surface of the workflow.

### Tines forSecurity teams

Learn more →

### Tines forIT operations

Learn more → [...] 750 daysof work time saved

Tines reminded us of Notion in that it’s highly flexible and customizable. It met our needs as opposed to forcing us to work inside the confines of the tool. And it met our needs for more than just a SOAR.

Ease of use and time to effectiveness for new users is insanely low.

10 hoursof work time saved every day

Beyond the product and product teams’ agility and speed, the level of support from Tines is the benchmark we hold all of our vendors to.

After a year, I have not come across something I could not accomplish in Tines.

An opportunity to bring automation into areas of the organization that just don’t have developers.

50% reductionin time spent per ticket
